text: This is a low-risk hedge against T-62. If labels become easier to discover and reuse, the pressure for heavier grouping concepts like parent/epic may stay low.

Related concrete bug/UX issue: see T-86 for the inconsistency where 'new -l a,b' does not parse labels the same way as update. Fix that baseline behavior before adding richer label-discovery affordances.

T-86 (label parsing bug) must land first — no point improving label discovery if the basic parsing is broken. After T-86, consider: tab-completion hints, 'did you mean X?' when a typo'd label matches an existing one.

Label discovery is easy with 'git toc labels'. We also now added a section of standard suggested labels to SKILL.md. That should cover the basics. If anybody ever writes a typo that shouldn't be too bad and probably will be discovered and corrected quickly. I'd rather keep the codebase small. T-86 also now is fixed. => rejecting.
